name: Onboard new customers with Jira and Microsoft Teams with instant alerts

parameters.content:
### Receive Inbound Webhook

Entry point: listens for POST calls on `/wf08623-task`. Use the TEST url while building, switch callers to the PRODUCTION url after publishing.

Next: Prepare Approval Packet

### Prepare Approval Packet

Shapes the item: writes `requestId`, `summary`, `requestedBy`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Request Approval via Microsoft Teams

### Request Approval via Microsoft Teams

microsoftTeams — `chatMessage` operation against the connected account.

Next: Hold for Decision

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Microsoft OAuth2

nodes.name: Note: Request Approval via Microsoft Teams

parameters.content:
### Hold for Decision

Pauses the execution until the resume webhook is called — this is the approval/HITL gate.

Next: Approved?

### Approved?

Two-way gate on `={{ $json.body?.decision || $json.decision }}`. TRUE continues the happy path, FALSE takes the alternate branch.

Ports: ①→ Apply Approved Change (Google Sheets) · ②→ Record Decline + Notify Requester

### Apply Approved Change (Google Sheets)

googleSheets — `appendOrUpdate` operation against the connected account.

Next: Join Decision Paths

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Record Decline + Notify Requester

microsoftTeams — `chatMessage` operation against the connected account.

Next: Join Decision Paths

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Microsoft OAuth2

### Join Decision Paths

Rejoins 2 branches into one stream (mode: append) so downstream logic runs once.

Next: Guard Non-Empty title

### Guard Non-Empty title

Drops items that fail the condition; only qualifying items continue (no false branch).

# Onboard new customers with Jira and Microsoft Teams with instant alerts

**Pattern:** Onboarding Sequence · **Level:** Foundation · **Trigger:** Webhook

## What it does
Onboard new customers with Jira and Microsoft Teams with instant alerts. It fires the moment an external system calls its webhook.

## Why it exists
Manual standups is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
Receive Inbound Webhook → Prepare Approval Packet → Request Approval via Microsoft Teams → Hold for Decision → Approved? → Apply Approved Change (Google Sheets) → Join Decision Paths → …

## Use it for
- Teams that want to onboarding sequence without manual effort
- Anyone automating standups in software development
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Responds in seconds
- Scales without extra headcount
- Saves hours of manual work each week

## Before first run
Connect credentials: Google OAuth2 / service account; Microsoft OAuth2.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## ⏱ Rate limiting

Work is batched and spaced with Wait so provider quotas aren't tripped. If you hit 429s, lower the batch size or raise the wait — throughput drops, reliability rises.

## 🔐 Security

Never trust inbound payloads: the webhook path is unguessable. Add header auth or IP allow-listing for defense in depth. Secrets live in n8n credentials — never in node parameters.

## 🙋 Human in the loop

The workflow pauses at the Wait node until a human posts a decision to the resume URL (included in the approval message). Approve → the action executes; anything else → recorded as declined. No timeout is set by default — add one under the Wait node's options if approvals go stale.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your standups process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
